title: Abort/`Fatal Python error` with `AtomicDict.reduce_count()`
author: devdanzin
body:
The following code will cause an abort:
```python
import cereggii
a = cereggii.AtomicDict()

a[1] = ""
a.reduce_count({1})
```

The error message is:
```
Fatal Python error: _Py_CheckFunctionResult: a function returned a result with an exception set
Python runtime state: initialized
TypeError: can only concatenate str (not "int") to str

The above exception was the direct cause of the following exception:

SystemError: <method 'reduce_count' of 'cereggii.AtomicDict' objects> returned a result with an exception set

Stack (most recent call first):
  File "/home/danzin/crashers/cereggii/atomic_dict_02.py", line 5 in <module>
Aborted
```

The backtrace doesn't seem informative.

comments.author: devdanzin
comments.body:
Found a similar abort, maybe fixing the first one will fix this one too?
```python
import cereggii
cereggii.AtomicDict().reduce_sum(((cereggii.ANY, cereggii.ANY),))
```

```
Python runtime state: initialized
ValueError: key in (NOT_FOUND, ANY, EXPECTATION_FAILED)

The above exception was the direct cause of the following exception:

SystemError: <method 'reduce_sum' of 'cereggii.AtomicDict' objects> returned a result with an exception set

Stack (most recent call first):
  File "/home/danzin/scratch.py", line 2 in <module>
Aborted
```

comments.author: dpdani
comments.body: Yes, I think they're the same error. Thank you for reporting this one too 🙏
